drafts.angle: The recruiting rejection spiral: athlete sends 30 emails, gets 2 responses, doesn't know why — until they see what D1 coaches actually want in a profile.
drafts.hook: You sent 30 recruiting emails and got ghosted by 28 coaches. Here's why.

script.visual: Close-up of phone screen showing Gmail inbox with red 'no response' notifications, athlete's face looking defeated
script.voiceover: You sent 30 recruiting emails and got ghosted by 28 coaches.

script.visual: Split-screen: left side shows generic 'Dear Coach' email template, right side shows phone with KickrIo profile builder open, athlete's stats and highlights properly formatted
script.voiceover: It's not that you're not good enough. Your profile is invisible. Coaches need to see your GPA, your speed numbers, your position flexibility, your highlight reel — all in one place they can actually scan in 10 seconds.

script.visual: Screen recording of KickrIo profile builder: athlete fills in height, weight, club team, GPA, best clips. Profile auto-generates a clean card.
script.voiceover: Build your recruiting profile once. Then every coach sees what they're actually looking for.

script.visual: Phone notification sound + coach response email appears on screen, athlete smiles
script.voiceover: The difference between ghosted and getting official visit offers? A profile coaches can actually read.

script.visual: KickrIo app icon on phone home screen, athlete tapping it

onScreenText.text: Link in bio to build yours free

shotList: Phone screen recording of KickrIo profile builder in action (athlete entering stats, uploading highlight clips)
Before/after split screen: messy Gmail inbox vs. clean KickrIo profile card
Close-up of athlete's face looking at phone (despondent → hopeful)
Notification sound effect + incoming coach email on-screen

suggestedSound.whyThisSound: Athlete pain point (ghosting) is the core of the hook, needs conversational, text-heavy vibe that feels like a peer dropping truth.

captions.tiktok: sent 30 emails, got ghosted by 28. turned out my profile was invisible to coaches. not anymore. build yours free (link in bio) #collegesoccer #recruiting #d1soccer
captions.instagram:
30 emails. 2 responses. 28 ghosts.

Then I realized: coaches can't see what they're looking for if your profile is scattered across emails and highlight compilations.

One clean profile. That's it.

Build yours free — link in bio.

postingNotes.bestTime: Mon-Wed 6-8pm ET (athletes checking phones after practice/homework)
postingNotes.cta: Link in bio to build your recruiting profile free (3 emails + 5 matches included)
postingNotes.coverFrameAdvice: The split-screen showing ghosted inbox vs. clean profile card — strongest visual contrast that stops scrolling.

drafts.angle: Parent perspective: your kid is a solid player, but you don't know which D1, D2, D3, NAIA schools to target — the school matcher cuts through the noise in 60 seconds.
drafts.hook: Your daughter's a good player. But which colleges actually want her?

script.visual: Parent (mom or dad) sitting on couch at home, phone in hand, looking confused, surrounded by college stickers and recruiting brochures
script.voiceover: Your daughter's a good player. But which colleges actually want her?

script.visual: Parent scrolling through college websites — takes 5 seconds per school, sighs, closes app
script.voiceover: You've looked at 40 D1 websites. Your kid's played in 15 ID camps. But you still don't have a real list.

script.visual: Phone screen recording: parent enters athlete's stats (GPA, club level, position, GPA, test scores) into KickrIo school matcher
script.voiceover: Here's what changes: you answer 5 quick questions about your kid's grades, club level, and position. The matcher shows you 50+ schools that actually recruit her profile.

script.visual: Results appear on screen — schools organized by division, match percentage, location. Parent's eyes widen, nods
script.voiceover: D1 schools, D2 pipeline options, realistic D3 targets, NAIA safeties. It's not guessing anymore. It's strategy.

script.visual: Parent smiling, showing phone to athlete (in background), both looking relieved

shotList: Wide shot of parent on couch surrounded by recruiting materials (brochures, printed emails, stickers)
Close-up of parent scrolling through 3-4 college websites quickly, showing frustration
Phone screen recording of KickrIo school matcher input form (GPA, club level, position fields)
Phone screen recording of results page showing 50+ schools in organized list with division badges
Wide shot of parent and athlete in background (athlete in practice gear or casual), parent showing phone, both smiling

suggestedSound.whyThisSound: Parent journey (confusion → clarity) is emotional and relatable; slow burn builds to relief when school list appears; piano conveys trust and wisdom.

captions.tiktok: we spent $3k on id camps, looked at 40 college websites, and i STILL didn't have a real list for my daughter. then we tried the school matcher. 50+ schools matched to her profile in 5 min. finally a strategy. #collegesoccer #parentlife

postingNotes.bestTime: Thu-Fri 7-9pm ET (parents winding down, checking phones before bed, planning weekend soccer stuff)
postingNotes.cta: Link in bio for a free school match (Family plan includes parent dashboard + AI coach email generator + kid's Pro features)
postingNotes.coverFrameAdvice: The results screen showing 50+ schools organized by division — visual proof that the app delivers a real list, not vague advice.
